# Transcrição: 01-The Customer Experience Framework

- **Fonte:** 01-The Customer Experience Framework
- **Duração:** 09:04
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:06]

How deep down the rabbit hole do you want to go in terms of improving site optimization and the custom experience? I've been doing this since 2005 and this is a deep field in its own right and would probably be deserving of its own training program. There's so much depth that you can go into of tweaking and optimizing, but for most people, the analysis that we'll cover here is enough. So understanding people's pain points and doing some data-based analysis, doing some tracking of what how users are interacting with your site will give you lots of clues as to how to improve the conversion rate on your landing page and through your funnel to give you better performance in Facebook. But the custom experience framework that I've developed over this period goes deeper into looking at many different parts of this. So like when I start off with analysis, I'm looking for the pain points, the clues, the data points that I can kind of build into my testing. So the reporting in the insights is many different ways that you can do this. Analytics is the most obvious. So in Facebook, you have the data on your site, most likely you'll have some analytics installed. So if you don't have anything installed at the very least, install something like Google Analytics, which is free, so that you can start to see some of the data inside your ad account and see what's working and what the opportunities are within there as well. Then kind of going a bit further into things like surveys. So as you start to generate leads, as you start to generate sales and you get traffic to your site, you want to ask more direct questions. So in this section, we talk about online polls and asking people if there's anything that's stopping them from buying or signing up today. You can extend that to things like net promoter score and asking customers after they've purchased your product or service and received it, whether they'd recommend other people as well. We use it ourselves for our training programs, for our e-com stores, because it's a great gauge as to whether you're on the right track. And it's partly, are you selling your product or service to the right people and are they experiencing the outcome that you promised them? It's a big part of building trust, building your brand and building your lifetime value as well. Things like customer service, satisfaction scores as well. So any of the interactions that your customers may have pre and post purchase, you have to track those and you have to see whether your customer service team are representing your product and service in the best light as well. Then we go into things like session capture. You can install tools which track the mouse usage on the page, how people are scrolling so you can create heat maps, whether they're clicking, etc. Building of personas. So we talk about 5W avatar. You can go deeper into business intelligence and your customer relationship management system to look at cohort data, look at how people that have purchased from you interact

## [03:06 - 06:07]

from that point forward. So things like lifetime value, how frequently are they buying from you? How recent did they buy? And once you get into the data, it's easier to then find opportunities to feed back into Facebook for example and create lookalikes. Landing page optimization, we'll be covering that off in this section. Looking at campaigns as well. One of the things that I like to look at is not just getting caught up in my ad campaigns and thinking about different opportunities to target people. It's also expanding out into what's happening around us. So if for example we're heading into a key holiday period like Christmas or Halloween or Easter or summer holidays, is there a pivot or an angle that my product can have there? So if I'm selling e-com or if I'm selling an info product, can I hook the message onto something that is around? Or maybe it's a sporting event or some kind of major political event. Main planning plays a big part into how you build your funnels, your landing page, etc. Creative testing goes without saying you need to be constantly trying new angles and ideas. Merchandising, so if you're running an e-com store, there are many areas of merchandising that you need to consider. So for example, what products in your categories are your top sellers? What is your stock availability like? So do you have enough stock to sell this product? And for example, if product is really selling well but you don't have enough stock, then maybe you need to take it away from the front end and surface something else and start testing different products. Behavioral targeting is an awesome way of really improving that custom experience by seeing their behaviour coming into the site and then personalising it and offering them a bit more of a tailored experience. So if they do certain activities before they land on your site, for example, come from a certain ad, you can tag that in your URL and change the experience on the landing page or maybe they're visited already or maybe they're an existing customer. Those small tweaks can make a big difference as well. And then you've got things like split testing, lab testing. For some people, it makes sense to have physical focus groups where you can go and meet 10 people and ask them about your product or you can do it online as well. And then you go into the whole learning phase. So this is all data capture. It's building on the custom experience and then into the learning phase and AB testing, split testing, which we cover off here as well, both within Facebook and on your sales fund as well. And then developing ideas into improving your custom experience. So it could be site structure, the information architecture on your site. So one thing I talk about when it comes to the types of user coming on your site, there's the hunters, there's the trackers and explorers. And your site has to tailor for these three types of people because for any number of visitors coming in, only a small percentage are going to be buying right now.

## [06:07 - 09:04]

So your site conversion rate could be 2%, 4%, 6% whatever. They are the people that have landed on your site and now are ready to buy. Then you'll have another 20, 30% of people who are in the kind of research and consideration phase, but they're not ready to buy. And then you've got a bigger number that are just not going to buy no matter what you do. And that's okay. But understanding that and then tailoring your experience around that makes a huge difference in funneling those people who are maybe that 60 or 70% that have clicked into your site, not ready to buy now, maybe not even next week or two weeks time. But you can build up that brand familiarity. You can build up that interaction with them so that even when they're ready, then you can target them with the ads at the right time and get them into your funnel based on your type of product and your kind of consideration path for purchase as well. And then you've got other things like additional functionality and landing pages, designs, products, promotions. So for example, you may have a lead gen funnel where you're capturing data and maybe running a quiz funnel. So you want to capture data and give them a different experience based on that. So for example, you know, if I'm selling a course on Facebook ads, I might have a quiz funnel, which grades their experience and gives them the right solution based on that. So you know, one might be asking them, you know, what is it look like? You know, what it is? How do you do XYZ? And then based on those answers, I might funnel them into a different product or a different funnel. Or for example, I might do a kind of data collection funnel where I want to find out like how much you're spending on ads right now, what's your goal? Are you doing e-commerce? Are you doing info products? That kind of data collection can then help me further down the line to tailor that experience and increase my conversion rate by speaking their language. So someone who's spending $100 a day on their ads and hasn't really made that much profit or revenue is in a different space to someone who's spending $1,000 or $5,000 a day on ads is making some profit but has instability versus XYZ. So like you can create those experiences and it comes back to your 5W avatar. Who are you targeting? What are their pain points? And how do you build the experience around that? So this whole section is all about getting more value from your traffic. So we talk about the key metrics that you can improve are, you know, write down from the top, return on ad spend is a factor of your average order value in your cost per acquisition. Cost per acquisition is a factor of your cost per click and your conversion rate. And the whole cost mix for this framework is about improving your conversion rate, impacting your average order value and then with the intent of reducing your costs. So this section is all about trying to get you better value from the traffic you're driving and convert as many people as you can with the highest value and the highest satisfaction to help your business grow.
